comment.body:
This is what I don't get on these maps. If the candles are moving up *despite* the positive charm (suppressive) as it nears the border the charm forces should get relatively weaker, thus less counteracting. This should make the buy orders encounter less resistance, and then charm goes negative so a rocket up. 
In other words, I wouldn't expect ceilings of blue borders. I'd expect movement up and "hitting" orange for resistance. Not up through orange and stopping at blue.
comment.raw_body:

comment.body: But we're not really seeing signs of a vol increase atm so the options market doesn't really seem concerned here. What would be concerning is if we start to see spot down vol down in which case SPX:VIX beta goes positive (we're at -1.5) and customers are long the risk reversal (sell puts, to hedge calls).
